selected_topic: NASA’s Webb Telescope finds methane on interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS
news_angle: NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope detected methane and unusual chemistry on interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS just two hours ago.
scene_metaphor: Hunk Mao as a funky space detective cat on a chaotic cosmic scene, wearing a futuristic black hoodie, sunglasses, and a cap tilted sideways. He’s holding a glowing magnifying glass shaped like a methane molecule, inspecting a comet that looks like a giant orange tabby furball streaking past with visible cartoon methane gas bubbles emerging. The comet sports a funky ‘strange chemistry’ chemical lab setup strapped on: beakers bubbling, strange alien petri dishes, and colorfully glowing tubes. Behind Hunk Mao, in the midground, a quirky space diner called 'Webb’s Galactic Gas Stop' serves interstellar snacks with methane bubbles instead of steam. The background shows distant planets and funky star charts resembling crypto graphs that subtly nod to the category. Tiny hidden props include a mini orange tabby astronaut plush toy, a crypto coin engraved with a methane molecule icon, and a chubby alien cat trying to catch methane bubbles. Text on a floating neon sign reads exactly: “METHANE DETECTED” in big block letters. The entire scene is vibrant, packed with details, and feels like an interstellar investigative adventure starring Hunk Mao.

image_prompt.composition: Square Instagram illustration with foreground (Hunk Mao detective cat in black hoodie, sunglasses, cap, and methane magnifier), midground (futuristic space diner 'Webb’s Galactic Gas Stop' with methane bubble steam), background (stylized planets, cosmic charts, and stars).
image_prompt.style: Youthful, energetic, colorful digital art with strong orange tabby motifs, dynamic poses, and expressive eyes. Mischievous confident smile on Hunk Mao. Packed with tiny visual easter eggs and hidden props throughout, minimal text (only neon sign).

caption: NASA’s James Webb just spotted methane and strange chemistry on interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS zooming through. This cosmic cat’s got his whiskers on the weirdest gases in the galaxy—methane’s not just stinky, it’s out of this world!
risk_notes: None - purely artistic interpretation with clear connection to verified NASA discovery. No financial advice or false claims.
